You have now prepared the modeling tools bundle with already installed plugins and predefined options.  </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740597 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00118: (2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed

- page_id: `272740597`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740597/2026x+Determine+the+Host+ID+or+Target+ID+of+the+machine+on+which+the+FlexNet+license+server+is+installed
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:22.240+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing > (2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

If you do not have FlexNet yet, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading Reader installation files' space=''] containing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) 3DS Media list for Readers 2024x' space=''], allowing you to set up FlexNet.

You can determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed:

- using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space=''] .
- using *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space='']*.** FlexNet license server supports only one Host ID/Target ID.

###### lmadminTo determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed (using *lmadmin*)

1. Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. The default address is[http://localhost:8090](http://localhost:8090/)or use your server name (*http://<server name>:8090*).
2. Click **Administration** at the top-right corner of the webpage. When prompted to sign in, enter the default administrator's credentials (*admin*/*admin)*.
3. Copy the Host ID/Target ID displayed as Ethernet Address (as in the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='ethernet_address.png']

###### lmhostidTo determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed (using *lmhostid*)

The *lmhostid* is a command line utility, which prints the Host ID/Target ID that a FlexNet license server requires on any supported system. The *Lmhostid* utility is available as an executable called *lmutil*.

1. Locate lmutil for a particular OS in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''] <*product folder*>\Flex_Utilities_User_Guides_Vendor_Daemons\<*FlexNet version*>\Server utilities folder.
2. Run lmutil with lmhostid parameter from the command line. Lmhostid displays the default Host ID/Target ID for the current platform.

For the exact commands for a specific OS refer to: License Administration Guide (Obtaining System Hostids):

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf']

If two or more Ethernet addresses are available on the particular machine, the first one printed by the Host ID command should be used.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740562 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00119: (2026x) Determine the Target ID

- page_id: `272740562`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740562/2026x+Determine+the+Target+ID
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:20.638+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To determine the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic concepts' space=''] of the machine on which the modeling tool is installed

1. Start the modeling tool.
2. When the License Manager dialog opens, click Show Target ID .
3. Select one of the following tabs based on the license server you want to use:
  1. FlexNet . Click to copy the text from the Host ID box.
  2. **DSLS**. Click to copy the text from the **Computer ID** box. Please note that more than one Target ID may be shown if you have several network adapters. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To determine the <ac:link ac:anchor="HostID/TargetID"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Target ID]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the machine on which the modeling tool is installed</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Start the modeling tool.</li><li>When the <strong>License Manager</strong> dialog opens, click <strong>Show Target ID</strong>. </li><li>Select one of the following tabs based on the license server you want to use:<ol><li><strong>FlexNet</strong>. Click to copy the text from the <strong>Host ID</strong> box.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>DSLS</strong>. Click to copy the text from the <strong>Computer ID</strong> box.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e05f7ab2-6409-4339-bb17-a9e29e64d2a2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Please note that more than one Target ID may be shown if you have several network adapters.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="determine_target_id.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Determine the Target ID" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol></li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00120: (2026x) Downloading installation files

- page_id: `272740376`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740376/2026x+Downloading+installation+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T15:05:44.354+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### 3DS Media lists

The topics below contain detailed lists of all the contents of the installation bundles for specific product versions, along with respective file locations.

The following list is not a full list of our product releases and the topics in the list do not contain links to software downloads. Use the topics below as a guide when you are not sure which installation bundle to download.

true

For the media list for Readers, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading Reader installation files' space=''].

##### Downloading software

The installation files are available on the 3DS software download page[https://www.3ds.com/support/download/](https://www.3ds.com/support/download/).

If you do not have access to the 3DS software download page, please contact your sales representative.

To download software

1. Login to https://software.3ds.com/ with your [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='3DEXPERIENCE ID creation and validation' space='NMDOC'] . By default, you will be redirected to **My Supported Software** .
2. Select CATIA brand.
3. Select No Magic product line. [ATTACHMENT filename='Step3_download_page_updated_pic.jpg']
4. Select Release (e.g. major version) and Level (e.g service pack) and Hot Fixes (if available).
5. Browse for required media files and click **Show details.** To find out the content of the volumes, refer to the [3DS Media list](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL/(2026x) 3DS+Media+list+for+2024x+Refresh2+release) page.
6. Click the download button or select the required volume file to start the download. [ATTACHMENT filename='Step6_picture_updated.jpg']

#### EXPAND: Up to 2021x

Up to 2021x

If you have No Magic login credentials, you can access the installation files, including the FlexNet license server utilities as well as the Demo and Reader versions of the modeling tool, at [nomagic.com](https://www.3ds.com/products-services/catia/products/no-magic/).

To proceed, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740537 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00121: (2026x) Downloading Reader installation files

- page_id: `272740537`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740537/2026x+Downloading+Reader+installation+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:18.892+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation > (2026x) Reader Mode activation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To download the installation files

1. Go to https://software.3ds.com/ and do one of the following:
  - Log in to the site if you have your **3D**EXPERIENCE ID .
  - Create an account if you do not have the 3D EXPERIENCE ID.
2. At the top of the page, click Free Download . [ATTACHMENT filename='reader_download_1.png']
3. Click CATIA Magic / No Magic . [ATTACHMENT filename='reader_download_2.png']
4. In the open window, read the license agreement and click the Accept the license agreement button if you want to continue.
5. Select Release (e.g. major version) and Level (e.g service pack). [ATTACHMENT filename='reader_download_3.png']
6. In the needed media files line, click **Show details**. The content of the media files is listed in the table below. To find out the content of the volumes, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) 3DS Media list for Readers 2024x' space=''] page.
7. Click the download button or select the required volume file to start the download. [ATTACHMENT filename='reader_download_4.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740445 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00122: (2026x) DSLS installation and licensing

- page_id: `272740445`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740445/2026x+DSLS+installation+and+licensing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:13.525+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server) is a licensing system developed by Dassault Systèmes that provides such benefits as:

- License usage statistics, debugging, and simple UI to administer and view statistics.
- Licenses are bound to expiration date rather than product version.
- Nodelock, floating, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managed licensing' space=''] licensing options.

##### DSLS and modeling tools compatibility

You **must** choose the DSLS version compatible with the modeling tool version you use.

| Modeling tool | DSLS version |
| --- | --- |
| 2024x | R2024x |
| 2026x | R2026x |

* To run DSLS 2024x licensing on Linux, glibc 3.4.20 (at a minimum) is required.

****IMPORTANT**. To use modeling tools with DSLS licenses,Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable must be installed (see[Latest supported Visual C++ Redistributable downloads](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/windows/latest-supported-vc-redist?view=msvc-170#visual-studio-2015-2017-2019-and-2022)). This applies to Windows OS only.

##### DSLS hosting restrictions

- DSLS servers can only be hosted on physical computers (with the exception of using HyperV virtualization; see the [Installation Guide](http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/R2024x/DSLS-installation-and-configuration-guide.pdf?_gl=1*zecu5a*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTcwMDEyMzUxMS40OS4wLjE3MDAxMjM1MTEuNjAuMC4w)).

##### DSLS licensing client-side main limitations

###### Supported license types

- Client on a physical machine - floating (online), floating (offline/borrowed), nodelock.
- Client on a virtual machine - floating (online).

- Offline (borrowed) licenses are not supported on macOS/Linux.

More information can be found in the DSLS server [Installation Guide](http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/R2024x/DSLS-installation-and-configuration-guide.pdf?_gl=1*zecu5a*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTcwMDEyMzUxMS40OS4wLjE3MDAxMjM1MTEuNjAuMC4w).

##### Product trigrams

With DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. To know the specific trigrams denoting each product, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Product trigrams' space=''] page.

##### Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server

The Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server is supported for DSLS licenses. Using the three-server redundancy capability, all three license servers operate to form a triad. The license servers send periodic messages to each other to make sure that at least two servers are running and communicating. A quorum is formed when at least two of the three license servers are running and communicating with each other. [Learn more about using DSLS triad license server >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Applying+Teamwork+Cloud+license#ApplyingTeamworkCloudlicense-UsingDSLS)

##### Detailed licensing information

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining license keys' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing' space='']

##### 3DS DSLS installation guide and software keys

- DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide
- Dassault Systèmes License Server and License Keys
- Software License Keys

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server) is a licensing system developed by Dassault Systèmes that provides such benefits as:</p><ul><li>License usage statistics, debugging, and simple UI to administer and view statistics.</li><li>Licenses are bound to expiration date rather than product version.</li><li>Nodelock, floating, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managed licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[managed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> licensing options.</li></ul><h3>DSLS and modeling tools compatibility</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55089c58-1713-4335-baa7-75adf8c5be7a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You <strong>must</strong> choose the DSLS version compatible with the modeling tool version you use.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Modeling tool</th><th>DSLS version</th></tr><tr><td>2024x</td><td><span>R2024x  </span></td></tr><tr><td>2026x</td><td><span>R2026x</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p>* To run DSLS 2024x licensing on Linux, glibc 3.4.20 (at a minimum) is required.</p><p>**<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong>IMPORTANT</strong></span>. To use modeling tools with DSLS licenses, </span><span>Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable must be installed (see </span><a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/windows/latest-supported-vc-redist?view=msvc-170#visual-studio-2015-2017-2019-and-2022">Latest supported Visual C++ Redistributable downloads</a><span>). This applies to Windows OS only.</span></p><h3><span>DSLS hosting restrictions</span></h3><ul><li><span>DSLS servers can only be hosted on physical computers (with the exception of using HyperV virtualization; see the <a href="http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/R2024x/DSLS-installation-and-configuration-guide.pdf?_gl=1*zecu5a*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTcwMDEyMzUxMS40OS4wLjE3MDAxMjM1MTEuNjAuMC4w">Installation Guide</a>).</span></li></ul><h3><span>DSLS licensing client-side main limitations</span></h3><h4><span>Supported license types</span></h4><ul><li><span>Client on a physical machine - floating (online), floating (offline/borrowed), nodelock.</span></li><li><span>Client on a virtual machine - floating (online).</span></li></ul><ul><li><span>Offline (borrowed) licenses are not supported on macOS/Linux.</span></li></ul><p><span>More information can be found in the DSLS server <a href="http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/R2024x/DSLS-installation-and-configuration-guide.pdf?_gl=1*zecu5a*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTcwMDEyMzUxMS40OS4wLjE3MDAxMjM1MTEuNjAuMC4w">Installation Guide</a>.</span></p><h3><span>Product trigrams</span></h3><p><span>With DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. To know </span> <span>the specific trigrams denoting each product, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Product trigrams" /></ac:link> page.   </span></p><h3><span>Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server</span></h3><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server is supported for DSLS licenses. Using the three-server redundancy capability, all three license servers operate to form a triad. The license servers send periodic messages to each other to make sure that at least two servers are running and communicating. A quorum is formed when at least two of the three license servers are running and communicating with each <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="65899956-f8df-483a-9359-c93f34c967d9">other</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Applying+Teamwork+Cloud+license#ApplyingTeamworkCloudlicense-UsingDSLS">Learn more about using DSLS triad license server &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><h3><span>Detailed licensing information</span></h3><ul><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining license keys" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><h3>3DS DSLS installation guide and software keys</h3><ul><li><a href="http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/R2024x/DSLS-installation-and-configuration-guide.pdf?_gl=1*zecu5a*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTcwMDEyMzUxMS40OS4wLjE3MDAxMjM1MTEuNjAuMC4w">DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide</a></li><li><a class="cf_div_theme_dark" href="https://www.3ds.com/support/documentation/resource-library/single/dassault-systemes-license-server-and-license-keys/">Dassault Systèmes License Server and License Keys</a></li><li><a href="https://www.3ds.com/terms/software-keys/">Software License Keys</a></li></ul>

## PAGE 00123: (2026x) FlexNet license server 11.12, 11.14, 11.16

##### Choosing the Flexnet server manager

There are three types of FlexNet server managers:

- *lmadmin* – a web-based license server manager with GUI (recommended).
- *lmgrd* – a license server manager with a command-line interface.
- *lmtools –*the old Windows-based server manager (replaced by*lmadmin*).

We recommend using *lmadmin*.

- lmadmin installer comes in 32-bit architecture (regardless of the version) only.
- 11.12.1 version license server utilities (except the lmadmin installer) come in 32/64-bit architectures.
- 11.14 version license tools are not available in 64-bit binaries. Only 32-bit tools are available (additional 32-bit libraries need to be installed in the 64-bit multi-arch system to execute them).

##### Prerequisites

- You have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''] the installation files of:
  - the FlexNet license server manager.
  - the vendor daemon - Cameo.

The license server manager (e.g., *l**madmin*) should be of the same or later version as a vendor daemon - Cameo you use.

##### For **Windows**users

To use *lmadmin* on Windows platforms, the Microsoft Visual C++ 2013 Redistributable Package must be installed. You have an option to install this package during the FlexNet Publisher License Server Installer process.

| Windows | 32-bit system | 64-bit system |
| --- | --- | --- |
| You can run lmadmin (11.12.1) on these officially supported platforms:Windows 8Windows Server 2012Windows Server 2008Windows 7 (Ultimate)Windows Vista (Ultimate)You can run lmadmin (11.14) on these officially supported platforms:Windows 10Windows Server 2008, including SP1, SP2, and R2Windows 8Windows 8.1Windows 7, including SP1Windows Server 2012 R2Windows Server 2012 | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin or 32-bit lmgrd with a 32-bit Cameo daemon vendor file. | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin. Additional 32-bit libraries need to be installed in the 64-bit multi-arch system to execute them. |

##### For**Linux** users

| Linux | 32-bit system | 64-bit system |
| --- | --- | --- |
| You need to have the glibc libraries installed corresponding to your operating system architecture.You need to create a non-root system user with root privileges and install/run the server tools using it:This can be achieved either by:1. Adding the user to the 'sudo' group2. Editing the file /etc/sudoers using visudo.Method 1 (Ubuntu based)Method 2 (Ubuntu based)Method 1 (Redhat/Fedora/CentOS) | We recommend using the 32-bit lmadmin installer or 32-bit lmgrd. You need to install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.For UbuntuFor Redhat/CentOS/Fedora | We recommended using 32-bit lmadmin and 64-bit lmgrd for 64-bit multi-arch systems.For version 11.12.1 we recommend either using the 32-bit lmadmin installer (for multi-arch systems), or the 64-bit lmgrd:Installing 32-bit lmadmin on a multi-arch machine will require 32-bit libraries.For recent UbuntuFor older Ubuntu useFor Redhat/CentOS/FedoraRunning 64-bit lmgrd on 64-bit systems requires 64-bit Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.For UbuntuFor Redhat/CentOS/FedoraFor version 11.14 we recommend either using the 32-bit lmadmin installer, or the 32-bit lmgrd for multi-arch systems. 64-bit 11.14 lmadmin/lmgrd binaries are not available.Both of them require 32-bit libraries. Install it using:For recent UbuntuFor older UbuntuFor Redhat/CentOS/Fedoralmadmin 11.14 comes with libssl.so.1.0.0 in its installation directory.You need to add these libraries in lmadmin directory to the LD_LIBRARY_PATH in order to run lmadmin.e.g. if your lmadmin installation is at /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager then adding it to the path would look like this: |

Check if LSB was installed successfully using:

```text

```

In order to query which lsb library is required for a particular Centos version, the following command can be executed:

It is the best practice to run license servers on a server-based OS.

Only 32-bit tools are available (additional 32-bit libraries need to be installed in the 64-bit multi-arch system to execute them).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Prerequisites</h3><ul><li data-uuid="4913ca16-3e90-45b5-9880-21ff42bc102b">You have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloaded]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the installation files of:<ul><li>the FlexNet license server manager.</li><li>the vendor daemon - Cameo.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="174dc4b8-b8b1-4c4c-9c06-fd091e6afc94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The license server manager (e.g., <em>l</em><em>madmin</em>) should be of the same or later version as a vendor daemon - Cameo you use.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />For <strong>Windows </strong>users</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="036ce8ad-72ca-4f4e-8dcc-e3cbcb0edccf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use <em>lmadmin</em> on Windows platforms, the Microsoft Visual C++ 2013 Redistributable Package must be installed. You have an option to install this package during the FlexNet Publisher License Server Installer process.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 68.6767%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 36.5338%;" /><col style="width: 30.3499%;" /><col style="width: 33.1164%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Windows</th><th>32-bit system</th><th colspan="1">64-bit system</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="d1b00959-81fd-4fd6-ae39-96fc86c825d6">You can run <em>lmadmin</em> (11.12.1) on these officially supported platforms:<ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p><span class="highlight">Windows</span> 8</p></li><li><p><span class="highlight">Windows</span> Server 2012</p></li><li><span class="highlight">Windows</span> Server 2008</li><li><span class="highlight">Windows</span> 7 (Ultimate)</li><li><p><span class="highlight">Windows</span> Vista (Ultimate)</p></li></ul></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="016d1425-ed3a-4ff7-8bb7-32b67017190e">You can run <em>lmadmin</em> (11.14) on these officially supported platforms:<ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>Windows 10</p></li><li><p>Windows Server 2008, including SP1, SP2, and R2</p></li><li>Windows 8</li><li>Windows 8.1</li><li>Windows 7, including SP1</li><li>Windows Server 2012 R2</li><li>Windows Server 2012</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></td><td>We recommend using 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> or 32-bit <em>lmgrd</em> with a 32-bit Cameo daemon vendor file.</td><td colspan="1">We recommend using 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em>. Additional 32-bit libraries need to be installed in the 64-bit multi-arch system to execute them.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><br />For<strong> Linux</strong> users</h3><table class="wrapped fixed-table"><colgroup><col style="width: 330.0px;" /><col style="width: 374.0px;" /><col style="width: 529.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Linux</th><th>32-bit system</th><th>64-bit system</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="919c71ad-d5e0-41ae-b75c-177faf5f3cb4">You need to have the <em>glibc</em> libraries installed corresponding to your operating system architecture.</li><li data-uuid="afc08c60-39ae-4ef9-b0cd-971972f11683">You need to create a non-root system user with root privileges and install/run the server tools using it:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84c7da53-aee5-4f15-96a8-b0f700800798"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This can be achieved either by:</p><p>1. Adding the user to the 'sudo' group</p><p>2. Editing the file /etc/sudoers using visudo.</p><p><strong>Method 1 (Ubuntu based)</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0bfb71-3afc-49f4-b7b3-f3fcb550ccf7"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo adduser newuser
sudo usermod -aG sudo newuser]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Method 2 (Ubuntu based)</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21f0d790-b7a7-44c7-ba6a-87007a2b1986"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Modify '/etc/sudoers' with the editor "visudo".

#Create a user called "newuser"
[root@mylinux~]#useradd newuser
[root@mylinux~]#passwd newuser

#Edit the file with visudo:
[root@mylinux~]# visudo

#Find a line 'User privilege specification' and add the following.
newuser ALL=(ALL) ALL]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Method 1 (Redhat/Fedora/CentOS)</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b31090f7-555c-4d1f-b83d-50a4e205ae4f"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo adduser newuser
sudo passwd newuser
sudo usermod -aG wheel newuser]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="561619de-1ac2-4a4a-905d-77ca4fadd140">We recommend using the 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> installer<em> </em>or 32-bit <em>lmgrd. </em></li><li data-uuid="8da398b4-2248-4eb2-92a5-d3f50075c984">You need to install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.<em><br /></em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5dcee0e-656e-424b-91b0-de927e44764a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>For Ubuntu</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198b5612-eea1-495e-be7b-fffc4e2cc4ad"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>For Redhat/CentOS/Fedora</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c23c6323-2d19-48ad-a145-3f478172143f"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="401b59d5-b046-432a-b170-dcb7c07cd7ce">We recommended using 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> and 64-bit <em>lmgrd</em> for 64-bit multi-arch systems.</li><li data-uuid="ae885820-3648-4896-b384-c0f610a7cb1a">For version<strong> 11.12.1</strong> we recommend either using the 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> installer (for multi-arch systems), or the 64-bit <em>lmgrd</em>:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab7a8433-756d-4aa6-b41c-a76ea798bd57"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">Installing 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> on a multi-arch machine will require 32-bit libraries.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For recent Ubuntu</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26501a78-51fe-4353-8961-a522f778ef53"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install libc6-i386
apt-get update
sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For older Ubuntu use</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="620a2b93-1e1c-4fa9-8492-01d23088ffe4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo dpkg --add-architecture i386
apt-get update
sudo apt-get install libc6:i386 libncurses5:i386 libstdc++6:i386
sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For Redhat/CentOS/Fedora</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d0df711-b712-4da0-90af-5a9994f0b8a5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core.i686]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">Running 64-bit <em>lmgrd</em> on 64-bit systems requires 64-bit Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For Ubuntu</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8dc2030-c762-467e-81db-1df29d50f353"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For Redhat/CentOS/Fedora</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0ad5ba6-9cdf-4e7f-b2e8-d134dd62a3e1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="59573587-15a7-421c-84ca-f7801e269f8f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For version <strong>11.14</strong> we recommend either using the 32-bit </span><em>lmadmin</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> installer, or the 32-bit </span><em>lmgrd</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> for multi-arch systems.</span><strong> 64-bit 11.14 lmadmin/lmgrd binaries are not available.</strong></li></ul></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77571cc6-10a4-45cf-9c03-a84ae8b62d35"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Both of them require 32-bit libraries. Install it using:</p><p><strong>For recent Ubuntu</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f82ddf73-bc7d-440e-8d23-95f3dfde5450"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install libc6-i386
apt-get update
sudo apt-get install lsb-cor]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For older Ubuntu</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aeb8e3ec-1d09-46ad-8c3b-ab5de76a0a1d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo dpkg --add-architecture i386
apt-get update
sudo apt-get install libc6:i386 libncurses5:i386 libstdc++6:i386
sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>For Redhat/CentOS/Fedora</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fdb54b8-cf99-4151-a47e-771a4c4cf0ed"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core.i686]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><em><br />lmadmin</em> 11.14 comes with <a href="http://libssl.so">libssl.so</a>.1.0.0 in its installation directory.<br />You need to add these libraries in lmadmin directory to the LD_LIBRARY_PATH in order to run <em>lmadmin</em>.<br /><br />e.g. if your <em>lmadmin</em> installation is at /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager then adding it to the path would look like this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87eef4ce-960f-4004-841d-e7bedd5a630a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$LD_LIBRARY_PATH:/opt/FNPLicenseServerManager]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>Check if LSB was installed successfully using:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6aaa2d3-43a3-4dae-91d1-2725008c240a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[lsb_release -a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3016a3-215c-478e-9b3b-4718b118c6ea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to query which lsb library is required for a particular Centos version, the following command can be executed:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2da32215-dd17-4353-a492-aaa69c96c8a8"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum provides /lib/ld-lsb.so.3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c226d4b-63d4-4f96-af9b-f6ac20daf7b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is the best practice to run license servers on a server-based OS.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="c867c190-3e4d-4a03-bab2-a8551318cf6b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing FlexNet license server 11.12, 11.14, 11.16" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="49acf52e-ab31-409a-9b97-19b974021f71"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Troubleshooting" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="dc07c0ea-8f2a-4b51-8821-8b1f0fc4b4b3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Useful commands" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

## PAGE 00124: (2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0

- page_id: `272740466`
- space_key: `IL`

#### INFO: Related Resources

Related Resources

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf']

#### EXCERPT: Method 2 (Ubuntu based)

INLINE

##### Choosing the Flexnet server manager

There are three types of FlexNet server managers:

- *lmadmin* – a web-based license server manager with GUI (recommended).
- *lmgrd* – a license server manager with a command-line interface.
- *lmtools –*the old Windows-based server manager (replaced by*lmadmin*).

We recommend using *lmadmin*.

##### Prerequisites

- You have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''] the installation files of:
  - the FlexNet license server manager.
  - the vendor daemon - Cameo.

The license server manager (e.g., *l**madmin*) should be of the same or later version as a vendor daemon - Cameo you use.

##### Windows

To use *lmadmin* on Windows platforms, the Microsoft Visual C++ 2013 Redistributable Package must be installed. You have an option to install this package during the FlexNet Publisher License Server Installer process.

| Modeling tool version | 32-bit system | 64-bit system |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2021x and later | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin or 32-bit lmgrd with a 32-bit Cameo daemon vendor file. | We recommend using 64-bit lmadmin or 64-bit lmgrd with a 64-bit Cameo daemon vendor file. |
| 19.0 and earlier | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin or 32-bit lmgrd with a 32-bit Cameo daemon vendor file. | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin or 32-bit lmgrd with a 64-bit Cameo daemon vendor file. |

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space='']**

##### Linux

It is the best practice to run license servers on a server-based OS.

- You need to have the glibc libraries installed corresponding to your operating system architecture.
- You need to create a non-root system user with root privileges and install/run the server tools(lmadmin/lmgrd) using it.
- You need to install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

###### Redhat/Fedora/CentOS

1. Create a new user and add this user to the group.
2. Add user to sudoers (/etc/sudoers).
3. Install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

```text
> /etc/sudoers
sudo yum install -y ld-linux.so.2
sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core]]>
```

Check if LSB was successfully installed using:

```text

```

In order to query which LSB library is required for a particular Centos version, the following command can be executed:

```text

```

###### Ubuntu OS

1. Add the user to the 'sudo' group
2. Edit the file /etc/sudoers using visudo. Method 1 (Ubuntu based) Method 2 (Ubuntu based)
3. I nstall the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

| Modeling tool version | 32-bit system | 64-bit system |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2021x and later | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin installer or 32-bit lmgrd. | We recommend using 64-bit lmadmin or 64-bit lmgrd with a 64-bit Cameo daemon vendor file. |
| 19.0 and earlier | We recommend using 32-bit lmadmin installer or 32-bit lmgrd. | Please use 32-bit lmadmin installer. Additional 32-bit libraries need to be installed in the 64-bit multi-arch system to execute them.Alternatively, you can use 64-bit lmgrd version 11.12.1. >]]> |

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space='']**

ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c226d4b-63d4-4f96-af9b-f6ac20daf7b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is the best practice to run license servers on a server-based library.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="095b902b-38ab-4905-bd3f-2827fb67f50e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Redhat/Fedora/CentOS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo groupadd -r lmadmin
sudo useradd -d /home/lmadmin -g lmadmin -m -r lmadmin
sudo echo "lmadmin ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL " >> /etc/sudoers
sudo yum install -y ld-linux.so.2
sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Check if LSB was successfully installed using:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6aaa2d3-43a3-4dae-91d1-2725008c240a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[lsb_release -a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3016a3-215c-478e-9b3b-4718b118c6ea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to query which LSB library is required for a particular Centos version, the following command can be executed:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2da32215-dd17-4353-a492-aaa69c96c8a8"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum provides /lib/ld-lsb.so.3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Ubuntu OS</h4><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0126fab2-10b9-46f2-addd-e2f6d5809318">Add the user to the 'sudo' group</li><li data-uuid="8d34ef1d-1c34-424c-898b-07b89334c965">Edit the file /etc/sudoers using visudo.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ba8d9ea-6775-4314-bd7d-4d932d3de324"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Method 1 (Ubuntu based)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo adduser newuser
sudo usermod -aG sudo newuser]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cca31eb-a3fb-4e77-b4be-f3002264a5f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Method 2 (Ubuntu based)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Modify '/etc/sudoers' with the editor "visudo".

#Create a user called "newuser"
[root@mylinux~]#useradd newuser
[root@mylinux~]#passwd newuser

#Edit the file with visudo:
[root@mylinux~]# visudo

#Find a line 'User privilege specification' and add the following.
newuser ALL=(ALL) ALL]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4bdff85d-13de-4a61-99f9-df5eb0f4b4d0">I<span>nstall the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198b5612-eea1-495e-be7b-fffc4e2cc4ad"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 75.8947%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 12.8525%;" /><col style="width: 25.5965%;" /><col style="width: 61.551%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1"><span>Modeling tool version</span></th><th>32-bit system</th><th colspan="1">64-bit system</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>2021x and later</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="e6c99d19-202f-4645-a72d-cdec7ebd09aa"><p class="auto-cursor-target">We recommend using 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> installer or 32-bit <em>lmgrd.</em></p></li></ul></div></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>We recommend using 64-bit </span><em>lmadmin</em><span> or 64-bit </span><em>lmgrd</em><span> with a 64-bit Cameo daemon vendor file.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>19.0 and earlier</span></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="f9c7af1c-ec65-4eed-b0f5-3e4ed6c6ac80">We recommend using 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> installer or 32-bit <em>lmgrd.</em></li></ul></div></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li execute them.</li><li data-uuid="117bfcb2-c6ec-4eae-be13-64eaf7d61476">Alternatively, you can use 64-bit <em>lmgrd</em> version 11.12.1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740458 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00125: (2026x) FlexNet license server 11.19.6, 11.19.8

- page_id: `272740458`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740458/2026x+FlexNet+license+server+11.19.6+11.19.8
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:13.974+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: ['lmadmin', 'lmadmin_run_as_service']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

#### INFO: Related Resources

Related Resources

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_19_6.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.8.pdf']

#### EXCERPT: Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)

INLINE

##### Version changes

- 32-bit lmadmin/lmgrd server managers are no longer distributed.
- 32-bit cameo daemon vendor is no longer distributed.

##### Choosing the Flexnet server manager

There are three types of FlexNet server managers:

- *lmadmin* – a web-based license server manager with GUI (recommended).
- *lmgrd* – a license server manager with a command-line interface.
- *lmtools –*the old Windows-based server manager (replaced by*lmadmin*).

We recommend using *lmadmin*.

##### Prerequisites

- You have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''] the installation files of:
  - the FlexNet license server manager.
  - the vendor daemon - cameo.

The license server manager (e.g., *l**madmin*) should be of the same or later version as a vendor daemon - cameo you use.

##### Windows

To use *lmadmin* on Windows platforms, the Microsoft Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable Package must be installed. You have an option to install this package during the FlexNet Publisher License Server Installer process.

| Modeling tool version | 64-bit system |
| --- | --- |
| 2021x and later | Use 64-bit lmadmin or 64-bit lmgrd with a 64-bit cameo daemon vendor file. |

#### WARNING: Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)

Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)

To use offline license borrowing with 11.19.6, the modeling tool 2024x Refresh1 version is required.

If you are upgrading from an older license server to 11.19.6 or later, users should return their borrowed licenses. Otherwise, a new license will be borrowed.

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8' space='']**

##### Linux

It is the best practice to run license servers on a server-based OS.

- You need to have the glibc libraries installed corresponding to your operating system architecture.
- You need to create a non-root system user with root privileges and install/run the server tools(lmadmin/lmgrd) using it.
- You need to install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

**Redhat/Fedora/CentOS**

1. Create a new user and add this user to the group.

2. Add user to sudoers (/etc/sudoers).

3. Install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

```text
> /etc/sudoers
sudo yum install -y ld-linux.so.2
sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core]]>
```

Check if LSB was successfully installed using:

```text

```

In order to query which LSB library is required for a particular Centos version, the following command can be executed:

```text

```

**Ubuntu OS**

1. Add the user to the 'sudo' group

2. Edit the file /etc/sudoers using visudo.

```text

```

```text

```

3. Install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

```text

```

| Modeling tool version | 64-bit system |
| --- | --- |
| 2021x and later | Use 64-bit lmadmin or 64-bit lmgrd with a 64-bit cameo daemon vendor file. |

#### WARNING: Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)

Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)

To use offline license borrowing with 11.19.6, the modeling tool 2024x Refresh1 version is required.

If you are upgrading from an older license server to 11.19.6 or later, users should return their borrowed licenses. Otherwise, a new license will be borrowed.

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8' space='']**

cameo.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="174dc4b8-b8b1-4c4c-9c06-fd091e6afc94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The license server manager (e.g., <em>l</em><em>madmin</em>) should be of the same or later version as a vendor daemon - cameo you use.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Windows</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="036ce8ad-72ca-4f4e-8dcc-e3cbcb0edccf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use <em>lmadmin</em> on Windows platforms, the Microsoft Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable Package must be installed. You have an option to install this package during the FlexNet Publisher License Server Installer process.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 49.8019%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.7846%;" /><col style="width: 71.2154%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><span>Modeling tool version</span></th><th>64-bit system</th></tr><tr><td>2021x and later</td><td><p>Use 64-bit <em>lmadmin</em> or 64-bit <em>lmgrd</em> with a 64-bit cameo daemon vendor file.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea531662-bdb8-468b-a5e1-85cf072b6e14"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use offline license borrowing with 11.19.6, the modeling tool 2024x Refresh1 version is required.</p><p>If you are upgrading from an older license server to 11.19.6 or later, users should return their borrowed licenses. Otherwise, a new license will be borrowed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="ForWindows"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><h3>Linux</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c226d4b-63d4-4f96-af9b-f6ac20daf7b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is the best practice to run license servers on a server-based OS.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="b9335aac-b42c-45a3-93cd-cd851c6e0ad3">You need to have the <em>glibc</em> libraries installed corresponding to your operating system architecture.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="ac9ebb74-757a-4d49-b4f7-34e2c043dc83"><p>You need to create a non-root system user with root privileges and install/run the server tools(lmadmin/lmgrd) using it.</p></li><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="409d3a74-3df2-428d-a094-78986537386f"><p><span>You need to install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.</span></p></li></ul><p><strong>Redhat/Fedora/CentOS</strong></p><p>1. Create a new user and add this user to the group.</p><p>2. Add user to sudoers (/etc/sudoers).</p><p>3. <span>Install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="095b902b-38ab-4905-bd3f-2827fb67f50e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Redhat/Fedora/CentOS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo groupadd -r lmadmin
sudo useradd -d /home/lmadmin -g lmadmin -m -r lmadmin
sudo echo "lmadmin ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL " >> /etc/sudoers
sudo yum install -y ld-linux.so.2
sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Check if LSB was successfully installed using:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6aaa2d3-43a3-4dae-91d1-2725008c240a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[lsb_release -a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3016a3-215c-478e-9b3b-4718b118c6ea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to query which LSB library is required for a particular Centos version, the following command can be executed:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2da32215-dd17-4353-a492-aaa69c96c8a8"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum provides /lib/ld-lsb.so.3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Ubuntu OS</strong></p><p>1. Add the user to the 'sudo' group</p><p>2. Edit the file /etc/sudoers using visudo.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ba8d9ea-6775-4314-bd7d-4d932d3de324"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Method 1 (Ubuntu based)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo adduser newuser
sudo usermod -aG sudo newuser]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cca31eb-a3fb-4e77-b4be-f3002264a5f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Method 2 (Ubuntu based)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Modify '/etc/sudoers' with the editor "visudo".

#Create a user called "newuser"
[root@mylinux~]#useradd newuser
[root@mylinux~]#passwd newuser

#Edit the file with visudo:
[root@mylinux~]# visudo

#Find a line 'User privilege specification' and add the following.
newuser ALL=(ALL) ALL]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>3. I<span>nstall the Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198b5612-eea1-495e-be7b-fffc4e2cc4ad"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 56.4861%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.167%;" /><col style="width: 76.8221%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><span>Modeling tool version</span></th><th>64-bit system</th></tr><tr><td><span>2021x and later</span></td><td><p><span>Use 64-bit </span><em>lmadmin</em><span> or 64-bit </span><em>lmgrd</em><span> with a 64-bit cameo daemon vendor file.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">  </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="678bfce8-84ca-49ae-a76b-4de17d0a362f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important! Offline licenses (license borrowing)</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use offline license borrowing with 11.19.6, the modeling tool 2024x Refresh1 version is required.</p><p>If you are upgrading from an older license server to 11.19.6 or later, users should return their borrowed licenses. Otherwise, a new license will be borrowed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8" /></ac:link></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="fb2914ca-e72b-432a-b831-ae8a511ccbea"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d744806a-084a-484c-a70a-c9cbad4f2c2e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Troubleshooting" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="51bd1756-d149-40d0-ac92-ee8ccc189d95"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Useful commands" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740451 space=IL version=2 -->
## PAGE 00126: (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing

- page_id: `272740451`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740451/2026x+FlexNet+license+server+installation+and+licensing
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-14T14:07:52.960+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### INFO: Related Resources

Related Resources

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.8.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_19_6.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf']

The FlexNet Publisher license server (the floating license server) from [Flexera](https://www.flexera.com/) is used for managing floating licenses. The basic components of the FlexNet Publisher license server are these:

- license server administrator
- license key file
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic concepts' space='']

A floating license allows you to install and use the same licenseon multiple machines.The licenses are managed by the floating license server. The floating license server has information on the total number of products and editions you have and how many of these licenses are currently in use.

##### Getting the FlexNet license server installation files

In your 'FlexNet License Server utilities' folder, you can find the FlexNet license server installation files, Administration user guides, and release notes. The folder is located in the appropriate media volume. To find out, which media volume contains the 'FlexNet License Server utilities' folder, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''] based on the release version.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space='']>]]>

##### Using the FlexNet license server

To use the FlexNet server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server' space=''] .

##### modeling tool and flexnet compatibilityFlexNet server and modeling tools compatibility

You need to choose the version of the **FlexNet license server** and a **vendor daemon - Cameo** according to the modeling tool version you use.

| Modeling tool\FlexNet version | 11.12.0 and below | 11.14.0 | 11.16.4 | 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2021x and later | not compatible | X 1 | X 1 | X |
| 19.0 and earlier | X | X 1 | X 1 | X 1 |

1 Windows 64-bit, Linux 64-bit, and MAC 64-bit ***lmadmin*\*lmgrd*** are incompatible with a**vendor daemons – *cameo*** of 64-bit. 
Use ***32-bit*** ***lmadmin*** according to instructions for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.12, 11.14, 11.16' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space='']

FlexNet license borrowing is not supported on macOS machines running with ARM Java.

The folder is located in the appropriate media volume. To find out, which media volume contains the 'FlexNet License Server utilities' folder, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DS Media list]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> based on the release version.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Downloading media volume files from the 3DS software download page >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Using the FlexNet license server</h3><p>To use the FlexNet server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9824c1a1-0864-4ed5-b357-c55ed830d02f"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install the server.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="fbb0f6da-0eca-4505-bab0-8f73fe5bcb6e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key to the server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f4c4a2e-65d6-48d5-a43a-b5799d3b3d0c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">modeling tool and flexnet compatibility</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>FlexNet server and modeling tools compatibility</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b86f952-fa01-457c-9af5-ffa2625e7c32"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You need to choose the version of the <strong>FlexNet license server</strong> and a <strong>vendor daemon - Cameo</strong> according to the modeling tool version you use.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th><p align="center">Modeling tool\FlexNet version</p></th><th><p align="center">11.12.0 and below</p></th><th><p align="center">11.14.0</p></th><th><p align="center">11.16.4</p></th><th><p align="center">11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0</p></th></tr><tr class=""><td><p>2021x and later</p></td><td><p align="center">not compatible</p></td><td><p align="center">X <sup>1</sup></p></td><td><p align="center">X <sup>1</sup></p></td><td><p align="center">X</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>19.0 and earlier</p></td><td><p align="center">X</p></td><td><p align="center">X <sup>1</sup></p></td><td><p align="center">X <sup>1</sup></p></td><td><p align="center">X <sup>1</sup></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><sup>1</sup> Windows 64-bit, Linux 64-bit, and MAC 64-bit <strong><em>lmadmin</em>\<em>lmgrd</em></strong> are incompatible with a<strong> vendor daemons – <em>cameo</em></strong> of 64-bit. <br />Use <em><strong>32-bit</strong></em> <strong><em>lmadmin</em></strong> according to instructions for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.12, 11.14, 11.16" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d984939d-347d-41e5-992d-d220510f0a75"><ac:rich-text-body><p>FlexNet license borrowing is not supported on macOS machines running with ARM Java.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740516 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00127: (2026x) FlexNet Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server

- page_id: `272740516`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740516/2026x+FlexNet+Three-Server+Redundancy+-+Triad+License+Server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:17.723+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the three-server redundancy capability in FlexNet Publisher, all three license servers operate to form a triad. The license servers send periodic messages to each other to make sure that at least two servers are running and communicating. A quorum is formed when at least two of the three license servers are running and communicating with each other. For more information, see the FlexNet Administration Guide on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space=''] page.

- Make sure the license server is using a defined port, the same one as defined in the license file.
- Three-server redundancy configurations require all three servers to use the same platform type.

##### Prerequisites

In order to run a license server in a Three-Server Redundancy mode (Triad License Server), you must first obtain a license key suited for it.

The license key must contain 3 SERVER lines with *hostnames* and MAC addresses defined.

##### Starting on a fresh installation of *lmadmin*

1. Have the cameo daemon in <lmadmin.install.dir>\licenses\cameo
2. The first server from the license key should be started first, by importing the license key from the lmadmin Administration tab.
3. After the import, the cameo daemon will state that it is "Down", and the lmadmin.log file will be displayed. This will happen until the second server is started.

```text

```

Licenses will only be served when there are at least 2 license servers running.

##### Starting on a *lmadmin* that was running a single license server

If *lmadmin* was previously started with a different, single server license, there is additional action needed. By starting a single license server, it goes into a single license server mode, and thus won't accept Three-Server redundancy (Triad) license keys.

There are two options:

- Do a clean installation of lmadmin.
- Reconfigure the current lmadmin and change the license mode.

###### Clean installation

To do a clean installation of *lmadmin*, first, you need to remove the previous installation. Then you can proceed with the fresh installation (see ).

###### Reconfigure the current *lmadmin*

Reconfigure the current *lmadmin* and change the license mode:

Steps to change server mode:

```text

```

1. Start lmadmin (using service). You do not need to do anything else after that. It will show Stopped until a quorum is reached (then it will start). Licenses will be served only when there are at least 2 servers running lmadmin (from the 3 defined).
2. The log file lmadmin.log , will show:

```text

```

Then you can proceed with the fresh installation (see <ac:link ac:anchor="Starting on a fresh installation of lmadmin" />).</p><h4>Reconfigure the current <em>lmadmin</em></h4><p>Reconfigure the current <em>lmadmin</em> and change the license mode:</p><p>Steps to change server mode:</p><ol><ol><li>In the <strong>Vendor Daemon Configuration</strong> tab, Stop and Remove the existing cameo vendor daemon.</li><li>In the <strong>Server Configuration</strong> tab, click <strong>Stop Server</strong> to shut down <em>lmadmin</em>. Wait for 1 minute for the server to stop.</li><li>Go to the <em>lmadmin</em> installation directory (e.g. C:\Program Files\FlexNet Publisher 64-bit License Server Manager11_17)</li><li>Open the command prompt in that directory (with Administrator user rights)</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type<em> lmadmin -import &lt;new_license_file&gt; -force -configDir &lt;config_dir&gt;</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff53967a-30bd-4fea-8f6f-e83b5b1b442c"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="_mce_tagged_br">This is an example (change the paths according to your machine):</p><p class="_mce_tagged_br">lmadmin -import &quot;C:\Program Files\FlexNet Publisher 64-bit License Server Manager11_17\licenses\cameo\triadKey.txt&quot; -force -configDir &quot;C:\ProgramData\FLEXlm\lmadmin11_17\conf&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">After executing the command, in the cmd line you should see:</p></li></ol></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="992525b8-51a5-47f8-846f-69d4d129a502"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Configured new vendor daemon, cameo.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Start <em>lmadmin</em> (using service). You do not need to do anything else after that. It will show Stopped until a quorum is reached (then it will start). root.Quorum is starting up. 
root.Quorum is waiting.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740555 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00128: (2026x) Floating licensing

- page_id: `272740555`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740555/2026x+Floating+licensing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:20.155+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) Basic concepts > (2026x) License types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### What is Floating Licensing?

FL (Floating License) allows you to install and use the same license (i.e. MagicDraw Standard, Professional, Enterprise Edition or Plugins) on multiple machines. Suppose you have 10 workstations where MagicDraw is used. In most cases there are less than 10 users concurrently using MagicDraw. Instead of purchasing 10 Professional (or Standard) seat licenses, only purchase the number of Floating Licenses to support the number of concurrent users. Floating License(s) are usually more economical in development centers than purchasing individual seat licenses.

##### How does license server works?

Whenever you obtain Floating License you may install MagicDraw client application (Standard - Enterprise) on as many workstations as you need. The license server software (which is free and is provided together with FL clients) needs to be installed on some computer (which is referred to as a license server). Normally, only one license server is required in your network.

The license server is provided with the key, which specifies how many and what types of Floating Licenses this server may serve. The same server may serve different numbers of different Editions and plugins licenses, e.g. 2 Standard and 3 Professional FL licenses. In this case, during the connection, you will be asked to select the edition you want to run - Standard, Enterprise, or Professional.

##### How does a client application obtain the floating license?

In order to work with the FL client, you will need to specify the address of the license server and port (by default, FlexNet - 1101, DSLS - 4085) and connect to it. Every time FL client connects to the license server it checked out a license from that server and the number of available licenses on this server is decreased. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the next client who wants to obtain a license must wait until one of the other client's finishes its work and the license is returned back to the license server.

##### What if the connection with the license server is lost?

Whenever FL client leases the license from the license server, it constantly updates the server with the message that it is still alive. The license server in turn acknowledges that the license is still valid. If the connection with the server is lost, the client application will not receive acknowledgements from the server and the client application will be gracefully terminated (MagicDraw forces you to save your work and close the application or try to reconnect again). In the case of no update from the client, the license server considers the license to be returned and it is available to other clients.

##### Floating licenses on a Laptop

Floating Licenses are primarily intended for workstations directly connected to a network. However, a portable computer that is connected to the Internet and can contact the license server, may use a Floating License as well.

##### Borrowing - offline license mode

If a license will to be used on a computer that is intermittently connected to the license server, that license can be borrowed from server and used as an offline floating license. A license can be borrowed from a license server for the selected period of time (default 24 hours) via Select Edition/Plugin in application. The borrowed license is used as an application on a computer that is no longer connected to the license server.

##### Node locking

Floating licenses are locked to a particular machine, on which the FLEXnet/DSLS server is running. The Ethernet address (also referred to as a MAC address) of Ethernet devices or NICs (network interface cards) is used as the Target ID for server license locking to particular hardware.

##### DSLS license server

For detailed information regarding DSLS, see the [DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide](http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/2023x/DSLS_Installation_and_Configuration_Guide_23x.pdf?_gl=1*1b9rvxw*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTY5MzIyODIzOS4zMi4wLjE2OTMyMjgyMzkuNjAuMC4w).

##### FLEXnet license server

###### License Server Manager

FLEXnet license server is used to manage floating licenses. The license server manager is one of the components that makes up a license server (the other being the vendor daemon). There is available a Web-based license server manager with the graphical user interface (GUI) - lmadmin.

A brief FLEXnet license server advantages and capabilities:

- Intuitive license management with rich graphical user interface provided by lmadmin.
- Easy to perform general license management tasks, such as configuring license and stopping servers.
- Easy accessible picture of license status - how many licenses are available and who is using them.
- Ability to report on the duration a product is in use.
- Alerts if licenses are running low, unavailable, etc.
- Allow/deny/reserve products to be used by specific users, hosts, and groups.
- Log file is generated, containing products usage information.

###### Structure of FLEXnet license server

*Vendor daemon* - one of the files that is a part of the license server. This executable is customized and built by us. To serve our products licenses, license server requires cameo vendor daemon.

*License file* - a text file, usually with the .lic or .txt extension, which contains license certificates.

*Options file* - a configuration file available on the license server, which license administrators can use to allow / deny / reserve products to be used by specific users, hosts, and groups.

*Report log file* - a file that runs on a license server, which contains data about the features used by a single vendor daemon. Report logs are encrypted and cannot be read by a person, but are used by FLEXnet Manager to produce reports.

*Debug log file* - a file used by the license server to record status and error messages that are useful for debugging the license server. Each license server can have one or more of these files.

*lmadmin* - the new license server manager, which includes a Web-based GUI.

*lmgrd* - a command-line-based license server manager.

###### License usage analysis

FLEXnet Manager is the only official solution with the direct access to the FLEX license usage information – enabling enterprises to track and manage their FLEXenabled software with the most comprehensive and accurate usage reports.

Use Flexera Software license administration solution, FLEXnet Manager, to gain exceptional visibility into license usage data and to create insightful reports on critical information like license availability and usage. FLEXnet Manager can be fully automated to run these reports on schedule and can be used to track license servers and usage across a heterogeneous network of server including Windows NT, Linux and UNIX. Contact Flexera Software at [www.flexerasoftware.com](http://www.flexerasoftware.com) for more details on how to obtain an evaluation copy of FLEXnet Manager for your enterprise.

In order to track license usage and gather license related statistics you might consider third party software also:

[http://www.openlm.com](http://www.openlm.com/) 
[http://www.jtbworld.com/jtbflexreport/index.htm](http://www.jtbworld.com/jtbflexreport/index.htm) 
[https://www.x-formation.com/license-statistics](https://www.x-formation.com/license-statistics)

###### Server configuration capabilities

The License Server options file allows the license administrator to control various operating parameters within the constraints of the license model. The user is identified by his/her user name, host name, display, IP address, and other. For the concurrent (floating) licenses, the license administrator can perform the following actions:

- Allow / deny / reserve products to be used by specific users, hosts, and groups.
- Restrict the number of licenses available.

For more information, see "Managing the Options File" section in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space=''].

###### Server alerts

The license server alerts when there are no available licenses or the server is down.

Alerts are configurable. You may set up lmadmin to issue alerts and warn you of potential problems. For example, you may set up to get alerts on license expiry or vendor daemon status.

Optionally you may use an lmadmin email alerter service. This service will poll for alerts and then send a user an email when an alert has been triggered.

For more information, see "Creating an lmadmin Alerter Service section" in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space=''].

###### References

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The license server in turn acknowledges that the license is still valid. However, a portable computer that is connected to the Internet and can contact the license server, may use a Floating License as well.</p><h3 class="blue">Borrowing - offline license mode</h3><p>If a license will to be used on a computer that is intermittently connected to the license server, that license can be borrowed from server and used as an offline floating license. The borrowed license is used as an application on a computer that is no longer connected to the license server.</p><h3 class="blue">Node locking</h3><p>Floating licenses are locked to a particular machine, on which the FLEXnet/DSLS server is running. The Ethernet address (also referred to as a MAC address) of Ethernet devices or NICs (network interface cards) is used as the Target ID for server license locking to particular hardware.</p><h3>DSLS license server</h3><p>For detailed information regarding DSLS, see the <a class="external-link" style="text-decoration: none;" title="Opens external link in new window" href="http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/2023x/DSLS_Installation_and_Configuration_Guide_23x.pdf?_gl=1*1b9rvxw*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTY5MzIyODIzOS4zMi4wLjE2OTMyMjgyMzkuNjAuMC4w">DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide</a>.</p><h3 class="blue">FLEXnet license server</h3><h4 class="blue">License Server Manager</h4><p>FLEXnet license server is used to manage floating licenses. The license server manager is one of the components that makes up a license server (the other being the vendor daemon). There is available a Web-based license server manager with the graphical user interface (GUI) - lmadmin.</p><p>A brief FLEXnet license server advantages and capabilities:</p><ul><li data-uuid="57a87b21-300b-46a3-a3c6-61ecc94d091e">Intuitive license management with rich graphical user interface provided by lmadmin.</li><li data-uuid="6484f62f-cd1f-4153-8c03-5f32b9b55429">Easy to perform general license management tasks, such as configuring license and stopping servers.</li><li data-uuid="41dad4a0-d28a-46b0-9808-8cb45d94b154">Easy accessible picture of license status - how many licenses are available and who is using them.</li><li data-uuid="afde1181-e84c-4cd6-b9e7-3a03928941dc">Ability to report on the duration a product is in use.</li><li data-uuid="ccb8ca1b-ba6a-473b-9d1a-2aa97ade69a7">Alerts if licenses are running low, unavailable, etc.</li><li data-uuid="0ff7eac1-0ebe-4b7e-a578-3b1c5c9c82ca">Allow/deny/reserve products to be used by specific users, hosts, and groups.</li><li data-uuid="ec64616a-da95-48b6-8165-52adcf3e9a1c">Log file is generated, containing products usage information.</li></ul><h4 class="blue">Structure of FLEXnet license server</h4><p><em>Vendor daemon</em> - one of the files that is a part of the license server.
