Report logs are encrypted and cannot be read by a person, but are used by FLEXnet Manager to produce reports.</p><p><em>Debug log file</em> - a file used by the license server to record status and error messages that are useful for debugging the license server. Each license server can have one or more of these files.</p><p><em>lmadmin</em> - the new license server manager, which includes a Web-based GUI.</p><p><em>lmgrd</em> - a command-line-based license server manager.</p><h4 class="blue">License usage analysis</h4><p>FLEXnet Manager is the only official solution with the direct access to the FLEX license usage information – enabling enterprises to track and manage their FLEXenabled software with the most comprehensive and accurate usage reports.</p><p>Use Flexera Software license administration solution, FLEXnet Manager, to gain exceptional visibility into license usage data and to create insightful reports on critical information like license availability and usage. FLEXnet Manager can be fully automated to run these reports on schedule and can be used to track license servers and usage across a heterogeneous network of server including Windows NT, Linux and UNIX. Contact Flexera Software at <a href="http://www.flexerasoftware.com">www.flexerasoftware.com</a> for more details on how to obtain an evaluation copy of FLEXnet Manager for your enterprise.</p><p>In order to track license usage and gather license related statistics you might consider third party software also:</p><p><a href="http://www.openlm.com/">http://www.openlm.com</a><br /><a href="http://www.jtbworld.com/jtbflexreport/index.htm">http://www.jtbworld.com/jtbflexreport/index.htm</a><br /><a href="https://www.x-formation.com/license-statistics">https://www.x-formation.com/license-statistics</a></p><h4 class="blue">Server configuration capabilities</h4><p>The License Server options file allows the license administrator to control various operating parameters within the constraints of the license model. The user is identified by his/her user name, host name, display, IP address, and other. For the concurrent (floating) licenses, the license administrator can perform the following actions:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e28a91ba-4bb1-4c0c-a564-aca282947dfe">Allow / deny / reserve products to be used by specific users, hosts, and groups.</li><li data-uuid="80963241-cd9c-4790-9130-48e161f31e88">Restrict the number of licenses available.</li></ul><p>For more information, see &quot;Managing the Options File&quot; section in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h4 class="blue">Server alerts</h4><p>The license server alerts when there are no available licenses or the server is down.</p><p>Alerts are configurable. You may set up lmadmin to issue alerts and warn you of potential problems. For example, you may set up to get alerts on license expiry or vendor daemon status.</p><p>Optionally you may use an lmadmin email alerter service. This service will poll for alerts and then send a user an email when an alert has been triggered.</p><p>For more information, see &quot;Creating an lmadmin Alerter Service section&quot; in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h4 class="blue">References</h4><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing and Running]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide (comprehensive server setup and management instructions)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740527 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: (2026x) How to change JVM version

- page_id: `272740527`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740527/2026x+How+to+change+JVM+version
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:18.336+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Java version support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change JVM version

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the <modeling tool>.properties file (eg., magicdraw.properties , csm.properties , cameoea.properties ) for editing. If you are using MagicDraw 17.0.4 or earlier, the name of the property file is mduml.properties .
2. In the JAVA_HOME line, add the path to the Java software. For example: JAVA_HOME=C\:\\Program Files\\Java\\jre1.8.0_144

integrations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To change JVM version</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.properties</em> file (eg., <em>magicdraw.properties</em>, <em>csm.properties</em>, <em>cameoea.properties</em>) for editing. If you are using MagicDraw 17.0.4 or earlier, the name of the property file is <em>mduml.properties</em>.</li><li>In the JAVA_HOME line, add the path to the Java software. For example: JAVA_HOME=C\:\\Program Files\\Java\\jre1.8.0_144</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e32030a-21c0-4de2-9f7f-49e2466f3a2c"><ac:rich-text-body>Integrated MagicDraw runs on the JVM specified by the IDE. In order to change the JVM, you must modify the startup properties for the IDE that MagicDraw integrates with. If you are running MagicDraw integrated with IDE, read the appropriate readme.html for specific integration, found in the <em>integrations</em> folder.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
